title: schizachyrium sanguineum subvar. hirtiflorum
intro: Schizachyrium sanguineum subvar. hirtiflorum, also known as Little Bluestem, is a perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its blue-green foliage and reddish-brown flowers. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Consuming Little Bluestem has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Little Bluestem is often combined with other plants to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal uses.
